body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mechanicality** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Mechanicality — habit as automated behavior, loss of conscious control, automaticity.
**Phase context:** Habit as automated behavior — loss of conscious control, automaticity, action monitoring.

**Distinct cortico-striatal compartments drive competition between adaptive and automatized behavior.** — PLoS One (2023). DOI: 10.1371/journal.pone.0279841. PMID: 36943842. Cortical and basal ganglia circuits play a crucial role in the formation of goal-directed and habitual behaviors. In this study, we investigate the cortico-striatal circuitry involved in learning and the role of this circuitry in the emergence of inflexible behaviors such as those observed in addiction.
